entry.title: BNAC vs CMMS: Rethinking the Role of the Asset Repository
entry.content:
Asset Repository Showdown: Why Business Network Asset Collaboration Leads the Way

Carlos Lemos, SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration marketing lead

When managing asset information, not all repositories are created equal. The difference between SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration (BNAC) and traditional CMMS (Computerized Maintenance Management Systems)/ERP repositories goes far beyond data storage — it’s about the future of asset collaboration in an interconnected world.

The Strategic Edge of BNAC

BNAC is purpose-built for today’s ecosystem-driven reality. Its power lies not just in storing asset data, but in enabling real-time, multi-party collaboration across the asset lifecycle - a critical advantage in industries where assets are commissioned by one party, operated by another, and maintained by a third.

What makes BNAC different - and better?

Ecosystem-Wide Visibility
BNAC operates as a neutral, cloud-native hub for asset data. OEMs, operators, and service providers can contribute to and access a single source of truth - whether it’s specifications, digital twins, or maintenance records.

Real-Time Collaboration Without Borders
Unlike static systems that update in silos, BNAC reflects changes instantly across all parties. This is indispensable during critical phases like commissioning, troubleshooting, or warranty claims.

Lifecycle-Centric Design
BNAC was designed to span the full operation and maintenance lifecycle of an asset — from handover, maintenance to retirement. It ensures that collaboration and context don’t end when the asset goes live — they evolve with it.

Faster Time-to-Value Across the Network
Because it’s cloud-based and shared, BNAC can be deployed quickly and scaled across a partner ecosystem, reducing coordination costs, system integrations and aligning all players.

BNAC isn’t just an asset repository. It’s a collaboration engine that turns asset data into a living, evolving asset narrative shared across stakeholders.

CMMS/ERP: Important, but Internally Focused

While CMMS and ERP systems (like SAP S/4HANA) remain essential for internal operational execution, their architecture is inherently inward-facing.

They excel at linking asset data to maintenance work orders, financial tracking, or inventory management — but within one organization’s four walls.

They offer detailed control and customization, but typically require manual updates or integration to connect externally.

Their role is essential in executing maintenance and financial processes, not in managing shared understanding across a supply chain.

Key Differences in Context

  • Scope: BNAC is outward facing, connecting your organization with their teams and with external partners. A CMMS or ERP is inward-facing, focusing on your internal workflows and systems.
  • Collaboration vs. Control: BNAC prioritizes multi-party collaboration. Prioritizing shared visibility. A CMMS or ERP prioritizes control and depth within your own domain, with collaboration typically limited to internal teams or manual data exchanges with partners.
  • Deployment: BNAC’s cloud-based, network-driven approach makes it faster to deploy across an ecosystem. A CMMS or ERP often requires more setup (especially if on-premises) but integrates more tightly with your existing tech stack.

Best of Both: Integration, Not Duplication

Forward-thinking organizations are increasingly adopting a hybrid model. In practice, many businesses use both. BNAC can serve as central and collaborative front-end repository, syncing ecosystem data into a CMMS or ERP (like S/4HANA via SAP BTP) for internal processing. For instance, an operator might use BNAC to make available asset information globally. Or an asset owner might use BNAC to access an OEM’s commissioning docs, then feed that data into their ERP to manage the asset’s lifecycle internally. This hybrid model leverages BNAC’s external connectivity and the CMMS/ERP’s internal depth.

In summary:

  • BNAC is used as the external-facing, collaborative repository to manage and enrich multi-party asset information.
  • CMMS/ERP systems are driven by internal workflows and financial tracking keeping full control over asset data within the organization

The choice—or blend of the two—depends on whether your priority is uniting an external network or optimizing your internal operations.

 

Summary: Why BNAC Should Be Your Strategic Front-End

In essence, BNAC’s advantage lies in breaking down silos between organizations, fostering proactive collaboration, and providing a dynamic, shared repository that evolves with input from the entire ecosystem. It’s less about internal management and more about external alignment.

BNAC can serve as an extension to your CMMS or ERP system. It is not just an operational add-on, but the strategic front-end repository that makes your internal systems smarter, faster, and more connected — and your processes more agile and efficient.

 

To learn more about SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration, visit us online.

entry.title: Developing a SAP FSM extension on SAP BTP CF using Federated Authentication
entry.content:
This article details a step-by-step guide for developing an SAP Field Service Management (FSM) extension on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Cloud Foundry (CF) with federated authentication.

It is not intended to be a "Best Practices" document, but rather a simple reference. Because it does not cover a wide number of potential scenarios, it should be considered just a starting point.

 

The use case.

When it comes to “Authentication”, to my experience, one of the first errors made when developing an extension for SAP Field Service (FSM) on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Cloud Foundry (CF), is to assume that since both are SAP products, authentication is implicit. Although it is reasonable way of thinking, it is not true.

Unfortunately, we learn very late, and the hardest way, that such an assumption is incorrect. Commonly, it happens when we have already generated the extension, it has an XSUAA (Extended Services for User Authentication and Authorization) enabled, and we are already trying to install it via the app SAP FSM Extension Management... just at the time the application throws an error that the extension is invalid.

After multiple attempts without figuring out what's going on, we probably will end up contacting SAP support. In most cases, they will recommend checking the Authentication section on the official SAP documentation portal or suggesting that the extension should be made publicly available on the internet. Which is often misinterpreted as a "please disable XSUAA" 😳

It's at this very moment where it looks like we're going to a dead-end road: if we enable XSUAA, we cannot install the extension. If we disable it, then our extension could be accessed from the internet by anyone. Additionally, looking at the official SAP documentation reveals there are several ways to achieve authentication, but none of them seem to align with SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Cloud Foundry (CF) 😥. Perhaps with frustration, we wonder if there is a way to get authenticated only in the FSM Shell, and then have the rest happen with Single Sign-On (SSO).

 

The solution.

In an scenario SAP FSM <--> SAP BTP CF, is possible logging in the SAP FSM Shell (only once) and letting the rest happen with Single Sign-On (SSO). This, thanks to the SAP IAS (Cloud Identity Services), which is a core component of SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) that provides authentication services and identity management in the cloud.

This blog describes the journey step by step, from developing the extension to configuring the Federated Authentication in SAP FSM, SAP IAS, and SAP BTP.

Before getting started, it's necessary to check the following prerequisites:

  • You have SAPUI5 development expertise: Advanced skills are not required but a basic level would be very helpful. You can find a variety of resources on the official website.
  • You have an account on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): If you don’t have an account, follow this tutorial.
  • You have an account on SAP Field Service Management.
  • You know the SAP Business Application Studio (BAS): Check out the official documentation or join the community.
  • You have set up the SAP Business Application Studio for development: If you have not set it up yet, follow this tutorial.
  • You have a DEV space up and running: If you have not created a DEV space yet, follow this tutorial.

This article is divided into five parts:

  • The development of the extension. Based on SAP Fiori design and SAP UI5.
  •  Integrating the SAP FSM ShellSDK library, which allows interaction between the extension and the SAP FSM Shell.
  • Configuring the Federated Authentication (using OIDC) between SAP FSM, SAP AIS, and SAP BTP.
  • The build and deployment in SAP BTP.
  • The installation process in SAP FSM.

 

Developing the Extension

a. In the DEV space in your SAP BTP Business Application Studio, choose New Project from Template:

b. Select SAP Fiori generator and choose Start:

c. Select Basic from the list of templates and choose Next:

d. Select None from the data source dropdown and choose Next:

e. Leave the default view name and choose Next:

f. Enter the module name, application title, description, and select Yes from the Add deployment configuration options. Then, choose Next:

g. Select Cloud Foundry from the target dropdown and select Yes from the Add application to managed application router options. Then, choose Finish:

h. In the workspace dialog box, choose Add project to workspace:

 

Integrating the SAP FSM ShellSDK library

a. To integrate the SAP FSM Shell SDK library into the extension, it is necessary to load it during the startup (for more information, see ShellSdk Examples). In your extension project, enhance the file /ui5extension/webapp/Component.js with the following code:

// Shell SDK library
sap.ui.loader.config({
    paths: {
        "sap/coresystems/fsm-shell": "https://unpkg.com/fsm-shell@1.20.0/release/fsm-shell-client"
    },
    shim: {
        "sap/coresystems/fsm-shell": {
            amd: true,
            exports: "FSMShell"
        }
    },
    async: true
});

b. Initialize the client by sending a REQUIRE_CONTEXT event to the SAP Shell SDK Host. For more information, see ShellSdk - Library usage sample. Adapt the generated code in the  file /ui5extension/webapp/controller/View1.controller.js, so it looks as follows:

sap.ui.define([
...
    "sap/coresystems/fsm-shell"
...

return Controller.extend("ui5extension.controller.View1", {
    onInit: function () {

        const { ShellSdk, SHELL_EVENTS } = FSMShell;

        const shellSdk = ShellSdk.init(parent, '*');

        shellSdk.emit(SHELL_EVENTS.Version1.REQUIRE_CONTEXT, {
            clientIdentifier: 'fsm-demo-plugin',
        });

...

c. To display some extracted data from SAP FSM, enrich the generated content in the file /ui5extension/webapp/view/View1.view.xml with the following code:

...
        <content>
            <sap.ui.layout:Grid xmlns:sap.ui.layout="sap.ui.layout" id="grid0">
                <sap.ui.layout:content>
                    <ObjectListItem title="account" intro="Account" icon="sap-icon://account" id="account"/>
                    <ObjectListItem title="accountID" intro="Account ID" icon="sap-icon://account" id="accountID"/>
                    <ObjectListItem title="company" intro="Company" icon="sap-icon://factory" id="company"/>
                    <ObjectListItem title="companyID" intro="Company ID" icon="sap-icon://factory" id="companyID"/>
                    <ObjectListItem title="user" intro="User" icon="sap-icon://account" id="user"/>
                    <ObjectListItem title="userID" intro="User ID" icon="sap-icon://account" id="userID"/>
                    <ObjectListItem title="locale" intro="Selected Locale" icon="sap-icon://globe" id="selLocale"/>
                </sap.ui.layout:content>
            </sap.ui.layout:Grid>
        </content>
...

d. Adjust the controller /ui5extension/webapp/controller/View1.controller.js by adding the following code:

...
...
  const oView = this.getView();
...
...
  
  shellSdk.on(SHELL_EVENTS.Version1.REQUIRE_CONTEXT, (event) => {
    const {
      account,
      accountId,
      company,
      companyId,
      user,
      userId,
      selectedLocale,
    } = JSON.parse(event);

    oView.byId("account").setTitle(account);
    oView.byId("accountID").setTitle(accountId);
    oView.byId("company").setTitle(company);
    oView.byId("companyID").setTitle(companyId);
    oView.byId("user").setTitle(user);
    oView.byId("userID").setTitle(userId);
    oView.byId("selLocale").setTitle(selectedLocale);
   });
...

 

Configure Authentication

a. In your extension project, create a file appconfig.json under ./ui5extension/webapp/. This concept is described in the SAP FSM Documentation.

{
    "name": "SAP FSM UI5 Extension",
    "provider": "SAP FSM",
    "description": "SAP FSM UI5 Extension",
    "version": "1.0.0", 
    "icon": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SAP-samples/fsm-extension-sample/refactor/samples/login-with-token/frontend/icon.png"
}

b. In the file xs-app.json, disable the xsuaa for the file appconfig.json as follows:

{
    "welcomeFile": "/index.html",
    "authenticationMethod": "route",
    "routes": [
    {
        "source": "^/resources/(.*)$",
        "target": "/resources/$1",
        "authenticationType": "none",
        "destination": "ui5"
    },
    {
        "source": "^/test-resources/(.*)$",
        "target": "/test-resources/$1",
        "authenticationType": "none",
        "destination": "ui5"
    },
    {
        "source": "^/appconfig\\.json$",
        "target": "/appconfig.json",
        "service": "html5-apps-repo-rt",
        "authenticationType": "none",
        "cacheControl": "no-cache"
    },
    {
        "source": "^(.*)$",
        "target": "$1",
        "service": "html5-apps-repo-rt",
        "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    }]
}

c. Get a SAP Cloud Identity Service Tenant:

  • In the SAP BTP cockpit, navigate to your sub-account Entitlements and check if you have an item Cloud Identity Services. If you don’t have it, choose Edit, then Add Service Plans. In the pop-up search for Cloud Identity Services, select the default service plan, and then choose Add Service Plan. Finally, save the changes on the Entitlements page:

  • Navigate to Services, then to Service Marketplace and select the item Cloud Identity Services. On the top-right corner, in the Meatball menú of the tile [...], choose Create.

  • In the pop-up, from the Service dropdown list select Cloud Identity Services. From the Plan drop down list select default. Then choose Next:

  • Leave the Instance parameters as Form (default value), and from the Service Type dropdown list select the value that suits your use case. For this blog, I have chosen Test.

  • Choose Next, review the configuration and choose Create.

  • After a short while, a subscription to the SAP Cloud Identity Service Tenant should appear in the list of Services ==> Instances and Subscriptions, in the SAP BTP Cockpit. You should also receive an email to confirm your account for the Administration Console of the Identity Authentication Service.

d. Set the Trust and Federation between the SAP Authorization and Trust Management Service and the SAP Cloud Identity Service:

  • In the SAP BTP cockpit, under Security ==> Trust Configuration choose Establish Trust.

  • In the pop-up choose the SAP Cloud Identity Service Tenant, then choose Next.

  • Choose the domain configured with the SAP Cloud Identity Services Tenant and continue with Next.

  • If needed, change the name, description, origin key, and link text for user logon of the tenant. Otherwise leave it as proposed by the wizard and continue with Next.

  • Review the configuration and choose Finish.

  • Set to inactive the Default Identity Provider: select the action item from the list, in the details panel choose Edit, uncheck the option Available for user Logon, then choose Save

e. Configure the Federated Authentication with OpenID Connect (OIDC), as described in the SAP FSM Documentation.

1. Register your SAP FSM Account as an Application in the SAP Cloud Identity Services Tenant:

- Open the IAS Admin UI, go to Applications & Resources, choose create, add a new entry as follows, then choose create:

- In the new Application, navigate to the section Single Sign-On ==> OpenID Connect Configuration. Choose a Name, maintain the Redirect URI, and choose Save.

The URIs depend on the SAP FSM cluster:

DE: https://de.fsm.cloud.sap/api/oauth2/v2/authorize

AU: https://au.fsm.cloud.sap/api/oauth2/v2/authorize

IE: https://eu.fsm.cloud.sap/api/oauth2/v2/authorize

US: https://us.fsm.cloud.sap/api/oauth2/v2/authorize

CN: https://cn.fsm.sapcloud.cn/api/oauth2/v2/authorize

- In the new Application, navigate to the section Single Sign-On ==> Subject Name Identifier. In the Primary Attribute Choose Email, and then choose Save:

2. Create an OIDC Configuration (SAP IAS and SAP FSM)

- In the IAS Admin UI, navigate to the new Application, then to the section Application APIs ==> Client Authentication ==> Secrets, choose add, maintain the new secret as shown below, then save.

Make sure you keep a copy of the generated Client ID and the Client Secret in your favorite notepad:

- In the IAS Admin UI, navigate to Applications & Resources ==> Tenant Settings, then go to the section Single Sign-On ==> OpenID Connect Configuration. From the details, go to the section Identity Provider Settings and copy the following Endpoints in your favorite notepad:

- Authorization Endpoint

- Token Endpoint

- Logout Endpoint

- Login to the SAP Field Service Management Admin App with your account. Make sure you have enough rights to maintain configurations.

- Go to your account details, navigate to OpenID Connect Configuration, then create a configuration as follows:

- Name: any name of your choice

- IDP Client ID: the one copied from the SAP IAS Admin UI

- IDP Client Secret: the one copied from the SAP IAS Admin UI

- Authorization Endpoint URL: the one copied from the SAP IAS Admin UI

- Token Endpoint URL: the one copied from the SAP IAS Admin UI

- End Session Endpoint URL: the one copied from the SAP IAS Admin UI

- Fixed Redirect URI: the one used in the OpenID Connect Configuration in the SAP IAS Admin UI

3. Configure OIDC as the default Authentication Method for your SAP FSM Account:

In the SAP Field Service Management Admin App, navigate to your account details, choose Edit, then scroll down to the section Security, in OpenID Connect Configuration choose the one created previously, and then choose Update:

4. Test your configuration:

- Have a "testing user" in your SAP IAS Tenant which is active and has the email address confirmed:

- Have the same "testing user" active in SAP Field Service Management with the same email as the one created in the SAP IAS tenant. The email address should be assigned only to this user, and the role SUPERUSER should be also assigned.

- Visit the SAP FSM Shell login page https://de.fsm.cloud.sap/shell/#/login, type in your account, then choose Continue.

- You should be redirected to the Identity Provider. Type in the email address of the "testing user", the corresponding password, and choose Continue.

- You should login to SAP Field Service Management.

5. In your sub-account in the SAP BTP Cockpit, navigate to Security ==> Settings, then to Trusted Domains, choose Add to register the domain of the Cluster where your SAP FSM account exists. For example, for DE it should be https://de.fsm.cloud.sap 

 

Build, Deploy, and Test the Extension

a. Build: in the SAP Business Application Studio right click over the file ./ui5extension/mta.yaml and choose Build MTA. Once the building is finished, the folders ./ui5extension/dist and ./ui5extension/mta_archives/ should be generated.

b. Deploy: right click on the file ./ui5extension/mta_archives/ui5extension_0.0.1.mtar and choose Deploy MTA Archive.

c. Test: make sure you have a subscription to an "application plan" of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition in the SAP BTP Cockpit:

Click on the extension:

It should open the SAP UI5 application (the extension) in a new browser. Make sure you copy the url in your favorite notepad, you will need it during the installation process in the app SAP FSM Extension Management.

Note that if you are using chrome, if you open the URL of the extension in a new browser in Incognito Mode, you should be redirected to the Identity Provider where you need to type in the email address of the "testing user", as well as the corresponding password. Then the SAP UI5 application should look like this:

 

Registering the extension in SAP FSM

a. Login to your SAP FSM account with your "testing user". From the Shell home screen, navigate to the Foundational Services app, then to Extensions ==> Installed, and choose Add Extension.

b. When asked for the Access URL, use the one copied in your favorite notepad while testing the extension on SAP BTP, then choose Next.

Make sure you remove the ending index.html from the URL. It should look something like this:

c. The extension details should be pulled from the appconfig.json provided in the extension. Choose Next Step:

d. Finish the installation process by following the next steps. For more information, see Manually Installing an Extension.

e. Assign the extension to the outlet position of your preference. For more information, see Placing an Extension.

The extension should be shown and it should have not required any extra authentication:

I hope it helps you improving your development experience, and if you have any comments or questions, please feel free to share it in the comments section.

entry.title: Re: Certificate C_S4CPB_2502
entry.content: This worked, however, I am unable to proceed because the user is locked.

entry.title: Kickstart your Cloud SAP GRC Learning Journey: Introduction to Cloud SAP GRC, its Submodules details
entry.content:
I would recommend referring my below earlier article on SAP GRC (On-premise) before you start your journey into Cloud SAP GRC. It helps you understand the basic concepts and how On-premise SAP GRC helps organization to achieve business goals.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/kickstart-your-sap-grc-learning-journey-introduction-to-sap-grc-submodules/ba-p/13997059

SAP Cloud GRC encompasses a range of robust solutions tailored to meet diverse organizational needs.

Below are some of the key offerings in the SAP GRC, public cloud solutions portfolio.

·       SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance

·       SAP Risk Assurance Management

·       SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, Cloud Edition

·       SAP Watchlist Screening

1.      SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance is a comprehensive solution designed to simplify user access management in the cloud environment. While it offers similar functionality like on-premises SAP Access Control, it is not a cloud version of SAP Access Control (On-prem) or SAP Access Control hosted on cloud. Instead, it is a new SAAS solution from SAP, built on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). It supports both on-premise and cloud systems.

Submodules/services:

·       SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, Access Analysis Service (like ARA)

·       SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, Access Request Service (like ARM)

·       SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, Role Design Service (like BRM)

·       SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, Privileged Access Management service (like EAM)

·       SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, access certification service (like UAR etc.)

 

Image Source: SAP

 

Common IAG and Access Control scenarios:

·       SAP Cloud IAG only - supports only Cloud systems

·       SAP Cloud IAG with Cloud Connector - supports both On-prem and cloud systems

·       SAP Access Control with IAG integrated edition (as a bridge) - supports both On-prem and cloud systems

·       SAP Access Control – supports only On-Prem and SuccessFactors EC.

Reference SAP hep portal:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CLOUD_IDENTITY_ACCESS_GOVERNANCE

2.      SAP Risk and Assurance Management

SAP Risk Assurance Management (RAM) is a comprehensive solution designed to document risks and controls, automate controls to safeguard financial and nonfinancial data, policies, and processes in the cloud environment.  This can be like on-prem SAP Process Control. However, it is not a cloud version of SAP Process Control (On-Prem) or either SAP Process Control (on-Prem) hosted on cloud.

Earlier it was called as “SAP Financial Compliance Management” solution.

It provides the predefined integration and content for SAP S/4HANA on premise and in the cloud.

 

 Image Source: SAP

Reference SAP hep portal:

https://help.sap.com/docs/risk-and-assurance-management

3.      SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, Cloud Edition

SAP Enterprise Threat Detection enables you to evaluate security threats in your IT landscapes in real time by leveraging SAP and non-SAP log data. It helps to identify, analyze, and neutralize cybersecurity threats with real-time SIEM intelligence in your SAP applications as they happen and before serious damage occurs.

This solution allows organizations to monitor and respond to potential threats in real-time, ensuring the security of your cloud operations.

Key features/modules:

·       Log correlation and analysis

·       Automated threat detection and alerting

·       Straightforward integration

 Image Source: SAP

Reference SAP hep portal:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ENTERPRISE_THREAT_DETECTION

4.      SAP Watchlist Screening

SAP Watch List Screening enables enterprises across all industries to screen the names and addresses of their business partners against sanctioned party lists, thus ensuring their compliance with guidelines, regulations, and legislation. To facilitate this task, SAP Watch List Screening is integrated with SAP Business Partner.

It helps in assessing potential risks on an exception basis helps avoid high-risk businesses, individuals, and entities.

This can be integrated with SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, as well as with APIs to extend to other systems.

A vital tool for screening against various watchlists to ensure compliance with legal and regulatory requirements.

Reference SAP hep portal:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_WATCH_LIST_SCREENING

 

I hope this helps to start your journey into Cloud SAP GRC also if you're aiming to level up your skills in Cloud SAP GRC ! By diving deep into these modules, you'll gain the knowledge and skills needed to excel in the world of Governance, Risk, and Compliance.

Please connect and follow me for the next upcoming informative articles in SAP Security, GRC, BTP, Internal Controls and Risk & Controls area.

Share your thoughts in the comments below !

entry.title: Alerting for availability status of a secondary db maintained in DBCO table
entry.content:
Problem statement

Maintaining a stable connection between the SAP system (ABC) and a secondary database (DEF), a Microsoft SQL Server, is crucial. However, any of the SAP kernel upgrade might require additional steps to patch the Database Shared Library (DBSL) due to SAP no longer supporting the SQL Server Express edition (SAP Note 3210043).

Background

Certain programmes require this connection to work, and these are maintained under dbacockpit > database connections. SAP doesn’t support the SQL Server Express Edition version anymore; we were made aware of this when the recent sap kernel upgrade replaced the dbmsslib.so library with a new version and it took a lot of troubleshooting to figure this out..

Testing the connection

Choosing and running the report ADBC_TEST_CONNECTION under se38 would let you know if the connection works fine

Attempts to Automate the Solution

Automating this seemingly simple problem is quite arduous.

Approach 1: Alert Setup via Solman or SAP System

Roadblock 1: Unable to find a method to set up alerts from SAP-to-DB connectivity in Solman or CCMS

Roadblock 2: One of the fundamental problems to address while configuring this from Solman was to check connectivity from sap-to-db and not Solman-to-db.

Approach 2:  Custom metrics in Solman can trigger an OS-level script, which then launches an SAP program to verify if SAP can still connect to the specified database.

Roadblock 1:  OS command console in sap solution manager has been discontinued from SAP Solman 7.2 sps 17, we can’t run scripts from OS level using DAA. (Sap note in references)

Roadblock 2:  Using SAP NetWeaver RFC SDK, that support executing programs via RFC in SAP systems often introduces multiple dependencies on Linux packages that need frequent upgrades. Additionally, SAP has stopped active development on tools interfacing with sap. (GitHub projects in references)

Approach 3: Using cmd line tools (like sqlcmd, isql) to connect to database possessed a challenge due to their inherent nature of connecting directly from OS and not via a sap system.

Approach 4:  ABAP development could’ve been an alternative, but this would require dev and testing efforts, also scheduling the job on the sap system creates unnecessary load.

Approach 5: After debugging the SAP standard report ADBC_TEST_CONNECTION, we found that several internal function modules cannot be called directly using SE37 and must instead be used as includes.

Roadblock 1: we were planning to write a programme that calls the function module through RFCs but then, there were other problems with this development approach as highlighted in approach 2 -roadblock 2

Final Solution:

The solution that we finally reached to was from sap 1644499, which asks us to set few variables and try connecting to the sql db using R3trans (R3trans uses the DBSL library for sql databases). As per the note, there will be an error message "4 ETW000 Connected to unknown DBMS MSSQL - not supported by R3trans", but this is not a problem, the connection is still made to the SQL Server. Along with this we get a specific authentication related error, but this again means the sap system was able to connect to db. With keeping in mind, the exact error code for sql db, we’ve prepared a script that returns a 0 value only if we get this code, otherwise 1. We plan to send a mail each time we get an error value of 1 and schedule this program via crontab.

Code:

#!/bin/bash

rm dbsl*
rm *.log

export dbms_type=mss
export MSSQL_SERVER=braagvcontrol01.duni.org
export MSSQL_DBNAME=DUMEtAdProject
#export MSSQL_SCHEMA=ZF
export MSSQL_USER=sapcon
export MSSQL_USEINTEGRATEDSECURITY=0
export MSSQL_PASSWD=xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
export MSSQL_DBSLPROFILE=1

R3trans -x > /dev/null 2>&1


if grep "18456" trans.log > /dev/null 2>&1 ; then
        echo 0
        # below line is a comment
        echo "Authentication failure" > /dev/null 2>&1
        # it's fine that authentication is failing but at least
        # the mssql server is reachable using R3trans protocol
       # command to trigger a mail to sap basis team would  be written here.
else
        echo 1
        # below line is a comment
        echo "Connection failed..." > /dev/null 2>&1
fi

References:

  1. 3132826 - OS Command Console in SAP Solution Manager
  2. 3140940 - [CVE-2022-22544] Missing segregation of duties in SAP Solution Manager…
  3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/relational-databases/errors-events/database-engine-events-and-errors?view=sql-server-ver16 – sql error codes
  4. https://github.com/SAP-archive/PyRFC/issues/372 - pyRFC
  5. https://github.com/SAP-archive/gorfc/issues/42 - goRFC

entry.title: Remote Debugging of SAP CAPM App - Nodejs
entry.content:
Step 1: Create a Sample SAP CAPM Application

To begin your remote debugging journey, start by setting up a basic SAP CAPM (CAP Model) application.

1.1 Initialize the CAP Project

cds init bookshop

This creates the basic folder structure for your CAP project named bookshop.

1.2 Add a Sample Data Model

cds add tiny-sample

This adds example files for the database schema, service definitions, and sample data.

1.3 Add a Custom Action and Handler

We'll define a custom action addStock in the service file (usually under /srv) and generate a handler file for implementation.

Add the action to cat-service.cds:

action addStock(ID: Integer, quantity: Integer) returns Books;

Then generate the handler file using:

cds add handler

Step 2: Deploying the Application to Cloud Foundry

2.1 Add the MTA Descriptor

Generate the mta.yaml file, which defines the multi-target app structure:

cds add mta

mta.yaml file for deployment

this file defines a structure of the multitarget app.

---
_schema-version: 3.3.0
ID: bookshop
version: 1.0.0
description: "A simple CAP project."
parameters:
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
modules:
  - name: bookshop-srv
    type: nodejs
    path: gen/srv
    parameters:
      instances: 1
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
    provides:
      - name: srv-api # required by consumers of CAP services (e.g. approuter)
        properties:
          srv-url: ${default-url}
    requires: []

resources: []

2.2 Build the Project

Compile and generate build artifacts:

cds build

Once the above command is executed we will have the below folders created in our bookshop app

2.3 Create the MTAR Package

mbt build

This command packages your app (db, srv, etc.) into a .mtar archive for deployment.

2.4 Login to Cloud Foundry

Login to Cloundry foundry via the BAS or VSCode using the below command.

cf login -a <api_endpoint> --sso

You'll receive a link to authenticate and retrieve a one-time passcode for login.

The api endpoint can be found as shown in below image.

this will give link to generate the temproary passcode for logging in in to cloud foundry.

once the above command is executed, a mta archive file also known as mtar file is generated, which we will be using for deploying the application to cloud foundry using the below command.

cf deploy bookshop_1.0.0.mtar

Important Note

To ensure that the deployment doesnt fail, ensure to make the authentication as mock in package.json file before running the cds build command.

{
  "name": "bookshop",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "A simple CAP project.",
  "repository": "<Add your repository here>",
  "license": "UNLICENSED",
  "private": true,
  "dependencies": {
    "@sap/cds": "^9",
    "express": "^4"
  },
  "engines": {
    "node": "^22"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@cap-js/cds-types": "^0.10.0",
    "@cap-js/sqlite": "^2",
    "@sap/cds-dk": ">=8"
  },
  "cds": {
    "requires": {
      "auth": {
        "kind": "mocked" // Using mocked authentication
      }
    }
  },
  "scripts": {
    "start": "cds-serve",
    "login":" cf login -a <api_endpoint> --sso",
    "ssh":"cf ssh bookshop-srv",
    "remote-debug":"cds debug bookshop-srv"
  }
}

Once the application is deployed, we can see the application in our BTP subaccount under cloudfoundry dev spaces.

Step 3: - Remote Debugging of SAP CAPM App.

Copy the app URL from BTP as shown in screenshot, to trigger the debugger via postman or chrome.

3.1 Establish an SSH Tunnel

Before we start with remote debugging, we need to use the below command to establish ssh tunnel[ basically secure tunnel to communicate with our app] between our app container in cloud foundry and VSCODE/BAS

cf ssh bookshop-srv

3.2 Start Debug Mode

In a new terminal, run:

cds debug bookshop-srv

If Chrome DevTools fails to open automatically, don’t worry. You can open the debugger manually.

chrome://inspect

3.3 Open Chrome Inspector

In Chrome, go to:

chrome://inspect

Click "inspect" under "Remote Target" to open the debugging interface like the below.

now trigger the request from postman

The debugger is triggered in window.

Happy Debugging !!

entry.title: DDIC Structure for Migration Cockpit Objects
entry.content:
Hello,

we are trying to export data from our sap system to another sap system using the CSV files generated by the S/4 HANA Migration Cockpit. Thes file format is based on specific table structures as seen in image below (in this example for the customer object):

The issue is that we cannot find any corresponding DDIC structures such as "S_CUST_GEN". Do these table structures exist within the SAP System? Or is there a piece of code where these structures are dynamically created from where one could copy them to a DDIC structure?

Thank you, Victor

entry.title: Show only manager item-comments in employee signature
entry.content:
Hi folks,

We are using item-comments in competency and goal sections. And we use the Other rating's tab permission to show managers and others the comments and ratings but not for employee.

This year we want to show in employe signature only the manager comments and rating (because is the final official rating) but not the other comments such matrix or manager's manager.

It seems that it is not possible even with the others-ratingtab-item-permission. 

Do you see another possible solution to show only the manager's comments?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Step By Step Configuring CTMS Setup in BTP
entry.content:
What is CTMS:

SAP Cloud Transport Management service lets you manage software deliverables between accounts of

different environments (such as Cloud Foundry, ABAP, and Neo), by transporting them across various

runtimes. This includes application artifacts as well as their respective application-specific content.

 

Architecture:

 

 

Prerequisites:

SAP Cloud Transport Management service can be accessed using a graphical user interface or using

programmatic access. Before you can use the service, the following prerequisites need to be fulfilled:

  • Using a graphical user interface

To use this, you need a subscription to SAP Cloud Transport Management in your subaccount.

  • Using programmatic access (API remote call)

To use this, you need a service instance that is used to call the export functionality of SAP Cloud Transport

Management. You must create this service instance in a space of the subaccount in which you have

subscribed to the service.

 

1: Configuring Entitlements to SAP Cloud Transport Management

            Entitlements are already enabled as per below screen shot.

 

 

 

2: Subscribing to Cloud Transport Management

              CTMS service is created using standard subscription.

3: Add Admin roles to my id in DEV Sub account

 

 

 

 

4: creating destinations in the Sub account in DEV Sub account

 

 

 

 

 

Name: TMS_DEV_IS

Type: HTTP

Description: Optional

Url: https://deploy-service.cf.<domain>/slprot/<myorg>/<myspace>/slp

 

Domain: Cloud Foundry API endpoint

Myorg: Org name

My space : Space name

 

 

Final URL (Depends on DEV BTP Details)

Proxy Type : Internet

Authentication: Basic Authentication

User: (Email id created in BTP)

 

 

 

Click Save

 

 

 

 

 

Create the same destinations for QA and PROD in DEV sub account.

 

QA: Destination

 

 

 

 

 

Production Destination in DEV Sub account

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

5: Create Transport Node and Transport Routes

 

  • You are on one of the following screens in the SAP Cloud Transport Management UI:
    • On the Landscape Visualization screen, you have chosen  Create a Node.
    • On the Transport Landscape Wizard screen, you create a transport node as part of the wizard.
    • On the Transport Nodes screen, you have chosen  Create a new node.

 

We will be using Transport Landscape Wizard for our effort.

 

Go to CTMS UI of Development

 

 

 

 

 

Assign below roles to my id.

 

 

 

Logoff and log back in

 

 

 

Click on Landscape Wizard

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

We are using DEV-QA and PROD and will be configuring Three-Node Landscape

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Scroll Down

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Click Finish.

 

 

Click on Landscape Visualization to view the Nodes and Routes as below.

 

 

 

 

 

 

6: SAP CI (Cloud Integration) à CAS (Conte t Agent Service) à CTMS Integration

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

            CTMS:

Entitlement,

Make Sure below Entitlements are created.

 

 

 

 

 

 

7: Create instance for CTMS

 

 

 

 

Service: Cloud Transport management

Plan: Standard

Instance Name: As your standards.

 

 

 

Click Create

 

 

Nextà Create Secret Key

Click on 3 dots on the right of Cloud Transport Management and select create Secret Key.

 

 

 

Service key: sk-clientid-alm-ts  (As your Company Standards)

 

 

 

Service Key Created

Click on Service Key to view the credentials.

 

 

8: Subscribe to Content Agent Service

 

 

 

 

Click Create

 

 

 

Instance Name: As your company Standards.

 

Create

 

 

 

Create Service Key:

 

 

Name: sk-clientid-content-ui  (As per your company standards)

 

 

 

 

9: Create Instance of Content Service Agent

 

 

 

 

 

 

Assign Content agent role collection to user.

 

 

 

 

 

10: Creating HTTP Destination for Content Agent Service

 

URL

Provide URL from service key

Proxy

Internet

Authentication

OAuth2ClientCredentials

Client ID

Provide uaa client ID from the service key.

Client Secret

Provide uaa client secret from service key

Token Service URL Type

Dedicated

Token Service URL

Provide uaa url from service key and add in the last /ouath/token

 

 

 

 

Click Save

 

 

 

401 Unauthorized can be ignored.

 

11: Create Instance and Service Key of CI Service

 

 

 

 

Next

 

Add above Highlighted role from drop down.

Create

 

 

Create Service key for process integration.

 

 

 

sk-clientid-secret-it-rt

 

 

 

Create Destination Pointing to CI Service

 

 

 

Field

Description

Name

Provide value as CloudIntegration.This value is case-sensitive.

Type

HTTP

Description

You can provide a description for your reference. This field is optional.

Provide the URL of the system that you want a create a destination to.The format is: <oauth → url>/api/1.0/transportmodule/Transport.

Internet

No inputs required

Token Service Password

No inputs required

Additional Properties

sourceSystemId: Enter the ID of the source node of the transport route, for example, DEV_Node.

Add Source System à Value (Source Node Created in CTMS)

 

 

401 can be ignored.

 

 

12: Configure Transport Mode in CI Service

Go to Integration Suite Application

 

 

 

 

Under Settings Tab Click on Integration (access is missing please add below role)

 

 

Log out and Log back in

 

 

 

Select Transport Tab

Click On Edit

Select Check Configuration

 

 

 

TMS Check Filed if you face the same please verify the CTMS Destination and update the credentials again.

And checked the connection again.

 

 

 

13: Next step enabling Transport option

 

Also, you need WorkspacePackagesTransport role to transport package from source tenant to target tenant. (To enable transport button in design tab).Below are the steps to assign the same.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Select Role -> Edit.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Finish

 

Next importing a test transport Package to make sure workflow is working as expected.

Once the test package is created (eg: 17 is the package created below)

 

Login to CTMS application and go to QA Node.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Select the package à select Import Selected

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Finish.

entry.title: Solving the ServiceInstanceName Dilemma: Token-Forwarding Destinations on SAP BTP with init_data
entry.content:
Solving the ServiceInstanceName Dilemma: Token-Forwarding Destinations on SAP BTP with init_data

When building multi-application landscapes on SAP BTP, configuring destinations correctly is crucial for seamless service integration. A common pattern is to forward an existing authentication token from an incoming request to a backend service secured by the same identity provider. The expectation is to avoid explicitly configuring authentication credentials or service bindings in the destination—just forward the token and go.

The Goal

We wanted to create subaccount-level destinations using the Generic Application Content Deployment (GACD) module in our MTA. These destinations should:

  • Forward the authorization token from the request (forwardAuthToken: true).
  • Avoid the need for service bindings or token service configurations.
  • Be configured with NoAuthentication, since the actual token is already included in the request.

The Problem: Our Initial Approach with GACD

Based on SAP documentation, we structured our mta.yaml like this:

modules:
- name: destination-content
  type: com.sap.application.content
  requires:
  - name: destination-service
    parameters:
      content-target: true
  - name: foo-api
  - name: bar-api
  parameters:
    content:
      subaccount:
        destinations:
        - Name: foo-api
          URL: ~{foo-api/url}
          forwardAuthToken: true
        - Name: bar-api
          URL: ~{bar-api/url}
          forwardAuthToken: true
resources:
- name: destination-service
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: destination
    service-plan: lite

The expectation was straightforward: deploy this MTA, and the foo-api and bar-api destinations would be created, correctly forwarding the authorization token.

The Unexpected Outcome: Missing destination property [ServiceInstanceName]

However, upon deployment, we were met with the following error:

[ERROR] Missing destination property [ServiceInstanceName] in destination foo-api

This error was perplexing. Why would ServiceInstanceName be required for a destination designed to forward an existing token, rather than obtain one from a service instance (like an XSUAA or IAS service)? This seemed to contradict the very purpose of forwardAuthToken: true in this context.

Clarification from SAP Support

After seeking clarification, insights from SAP Development Support shed light on the situation. Here are the key points:

  • The GACD destination scenario, as currently implemented, primarily supports creating destinations through a service instance.
  • This means that even with forwardAuthToken: true, the GACD content module expects a ServiceInstanceName property. This implies an implicit dependency on a service instance for destination configuration, even if not for token fetching in the runtime.
  • For creating "NoAuthentication" destinations that simply forward the authorization token and do not rely on the destination service to obtain one from a bound service instance, the init_data approach is the recommended method.

The Recommended Solution: Using init_data

The init_data configuration within the resources section of your mta.yaml allows for direct definition of destination properties, bypassing the GACD content module's specific requirements for ServiceInstanceName in this `forwardAuthToken` scenario.

Here are corrected examples demonstrating how to achieve this using init_data:

Example 1: Creating my_destination_service-1

_schema-version: 3.1.0
ID: destination-with-app-1
version: 4.0.0

modules:
- name: app-provides-url-1
  type: staticfile
  path: ./hello-world.zip
  parameters:
    memory: 64M
  provides:
  - name: srv-api-1
    properties:
      srv-url: ${default-url}

resources:
  - name: my_destination_service
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    requires:
      - name: srv-api-1
    parameters:
      service-plan: lite
      service: destination
      config:
        HTML5Runtime_enabled: true
        init_data:
          subaccount:
            existing_destinations_policy: update
            destinations:
              - Authentication: NoAuthentication
                HTML5.ForwardAuthToken: true
                Name: my_destination_service-1
                ProxyType: Internet
                Type: HTTP
                URL: ~{srv-api-1/srv-url}

Example 2: Appending my_destination_service-2 (reusing the same destination service instance)

_schema-version: 3.1.0
ID: destination-with-app-2
version: 4.0.0

modules:
- name: app-provides-url-2
  type: staticfile
  path: ./hello-world.zip
  parameters:
    memory: 64M
  provides:
  - name: srv-api-2
    properties:
      srv-url: ${default-url}

resources:
  - name: my_destination_service
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    requires:
      - name: srv-api-2
    parameters:
      service-plan: lite
      service: destination
      config:
        HTML5Runtime_enabled: true
        init_data:
          subaccount:
            existing_destinations_policy: update
            destinations:
              - Authentication: NoAuthentication
                HTML5.ForwardAuthToken: true
                Name: my_destination_service-2 # Name must be different from existing destinations
                ProxyType: Internet
                Type: HTTP
                URL: ~{srv-api-2/srv-url}

Key Highlights for `init_data` Destinations:

  • Authentication: NoAuthentication: Explicitly states that the destination itself will not handle authentication.
  • HTML5.ForwardAuthToken: true: This specific property (often used in the context of HTML5 applications and Approuter) instructs the runtime to forward the incoming authorization token.
  • No ServiceInstanceName Needed: When defined via `init_data` in this manner, you avoid the GACD-imposed requirement for this property.
  • Direct Configuration: The destination is configured directly as part of the destination service instance's parameters, rather than through a separate `com.sap.application.content` module.

Understanding Resource Reuse and Destination Merging

A crucial point highlighted was how the my_destination_service resource behaves across multiple MTA deployments. When a resource with the same name and type (org.cloudfoundry.managed-service) is defined in several MTAs:

  • The system creates the service instance with the initialization data from the first deployment.
  • Subsequent deployments of other MTAs defining the same resource will update this existing service instance. Their init_data will be incorporated, effectively merging the destinations.
  • Crucially, existing destinations (with different names) will not be deleted. New destinations are appended to the existing set. This "append" behavior is governed by the existing_destinations_policy: update setting within the init_data.

This means that if you deploy "destination-with-app-1" first, my_destination_service is created with my_destination_service-1. If you then deploy "destination-with-app-2" (using the same my_destination_service resource name), my_destination_service-2 will be added, resulting in both destinations existing within the same my_destination_service instance.

Conclusion

While the GACD content module offers a streamlined way to deploy destinations alongside application content, its current limitations prevent it from directly supporting simple token-forwarding destinations without a mandatory `ServiceInstanceName`. For now, leveraging the init_data configuration provides the flexibility to define these "NoAuthentication" destinations, ensuring that the authorization token is forwarded as intended without unnecessary dependencies.

Understanding the nuances of how service instances and their init_data are managed across MTA deployments is key to building robust and scalable solutions on SAP BTP. We hope to see future enhancements to the GACD module to more intuitively support this common and valuable pattern.

entry.title: Re: PowerDesigner Repository on Azure SQL Database
entry.content:
Hello,

Microsoft SQL Azure Database is not currently supported for installing the repository.
The list of supported DBMS can be found in the installation guide.

A feature request is open under CR 790387 to address this inquiry and it is documented in KBA 0002223355 - Repository on Microsoft Azure and Amazon Cloud.
Please mark KBA 0002223355 as a Favorite by clicking on the Star icon to receive an update when the new feature is implemented.

Kind regards

In such case you would need to check full log and verify if there are any errors or exceptions.

To enable more logs for rush build you might check project.properties of npm-ancillary extension and adjust following properties: npmancillary.rush.build.dev.mode=false

Enabling dev.mode and changing loglevel should give you more information on occurring issue.

entry.title: Re: SAC Story hierarchy Node level data change
entry.content:
@yoursraghu986 

I will assume you do not want to change the underlying data but want to calculate retained earnings differently then what is coming from S4. You could create a calculated account in the Account dimension with formula of your choice for calculating the output. You can then create an alternate hierarchy where you include this newly calculated account instead of the old node.

Its not possible and also not advisable to override the aggregation behavior of existing node.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Overwrite dimension filters in SAC Add-in with date r...
entry.content:
@Jonas89 

SAP.OVERWRITEDIMENSIONFILTER does not support specifying the range directly in the formula. You could try putting the values in an Excel cell range and then refer the cell range in the formula. For e.g  cell A1:A10, you add the values and then refer this is the formula. With dates its tricky as first you need to generate the full range of values in Excel cell range.

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: 交易幣別與功能幣別的匯率只能預設抓取IMG的設定，是否有方法讓USER自行調整匯率?
entry.content:
Hello @Fang ,

1. 目前在Public Cloud版本中，公司代码货币不支持调整

2. Functional Currency的汇率转换逻辑确实是从配置中定义的汇率类型决定的，通过汇率类型找到对应汇率取数并计算于Functional Currency的金额。在入账的时候，不支持直接输入汇率供Transactional Currency到Functional Currency的转换。可以考虑是否能通过汇率上传/API每日更新汇率作为workaround处理。

Kind Regards,

entry.title: Re: Facing issue while deploying a UI5 application
entry.content:
Hi @Fabrice , here is the mta file:

_schema-version: "3.2"
ID: ordersmgtnspworkflowuimodule
description: Generated by Fiori Tools
version: 0.0.1
modules:
- name: ordersmgtnspworkflowuimodule-app-content
  type: com.sap.application.content
  path: .
  requires:
  - name: ordersmgtnspworkflowuimodule-repo-host
    parameters:
      content-target: true
  build-parameters:
    build-result: resources
    requires:
    - artifacts:
      - ordersmgtnspworkflowuimodule.zip
      name: ordersmgtnspworkflowuimodule
      target-path: resources/
- name: ordersmgtnspworkflowuimodule
  type: html5
  path: .
  build-parameters:
    build-result: dist
    builder: custom
    commands:
    - npm install
    - npm run build:cf
    supported-platforms: []
resources:
- name: ordersmgtnspworkflowuimodule-repo-host
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: html5-apps-repo
    service-name: ordersmgtnspworkflowuimodule-html5-service
    service-plan: app-host
- name: ordersmgtnspworkflowuimodule-destination-service
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    config:
      HTML5Runtime_enabled: false
      init_data:
        instance:
          destinations:
          - Authentication: NoAuthentication
            Name: ui5
            ProxyType: Internet
            Type: HTTP
            URL: https://ui5.sap.com
          existing_destinations_policy: update
      version: 1.0.0
    service: destination
    service-name: ordersmgtnspworkflowuimodule-destination-service
    service-plan: lite
parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo
  enable-parallel-deployments: true

entry.title: SAC vs Tableau vs Power BI
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I need to identify the advantages that SAC has over other leading analytics applications.

Gartner has suggested the following alternatives to SAC within the SAP Business Data Cloud. Unfortunately, I am not convinced by any official explanations of their features, so I cannot strongly recommend SAC. Tableau, Power BI and other applications also offer excellent connectivity with SAP for graphical, real-time and AI-powered analysis. I am finding it difficult to identify any significant differences. Even if we consider SAC planning as a unique function, there could be better alternatives to SAC, or just utilizing BPC.

Could you share your thoughts on what actually sets them apart based on your experience? Any thoughts would be appreciated. What should we focus on when choosing from these applications?

  • Tableau
  • Microsoft Power BI
  • Qlik Sense
  • Strategy One
  • SAP BusinessObjects BI Suite
  • Amazon QuickSight
  • Looker
  • Sisense Fusion Analytics

SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Re: SAP Business ByDesign - OData API Examples
entry.content:
Hello @gerdb42 ,

you will need to modify the ODATA if not done already.

Go to Function Import tab
Selete the query - querybyElements row
Under the parameters table
Click on Add row
and then add ArrivalDateTime
SAve and activate
After this, you should be able to query

/sap/byd/odata/cust/v1/khconfirmedinbounddelivery/QueryByElements?$expand=ArrivalPeriod&$filter=ArrivalPeriod/StartDateTime ge datetimeoffset'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' and ArrivalPeriod/StartDateTime le datetimeoffset'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ'

entry.title: Rework required for Fiori apps exposed in SAP Workzone containing calls to external data sources?
entry.content:
Dear community,
I have this problem with a Fiori app exposed in SAP Workzone. The app contains a call to an archive server to fetch image data (of a scanned invoice). The app works fine when the user is logged in to the company intranet, but is not able to retrieve the image when logged on only to SAP Workzone, because the user's browser cannot reach the archive server (which is not exposed to Internet).

It seems to me that the only way to make the app work in SAP Workzone is to replace the client-side call to the external data source with a server-side call. That way the SAP Back-end would execute the call, fetch the data and push it to the SAP Workzone user.

In general this code rework should be done for all Fiori apps that use external (not part of the back-end) data sources. 
Or am I wrong here?

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps - Set App Variable
entry.content:
When you click on the "Set App Variable" logic function, on the right panel, you would see "Variable name" and "Assigned Value". In the "Assigned value", you have to choose "Formula" option. A formula editor pop up shows up. You can use the WITH_ITEM formula for this case since you want to append an item to a list. The item should be of the same data type as the list elements.

For eg: Lets say you have a list1(list of objects) which contains the items displayed on the overview page and you want to add item2 to list1 on the click of a button, the formula you would use in the formula editor would be:

WITH_ITEM(appVars.list1, item2) -> change the list1, item2 according to your variable names

The Formula editor shows how WITH_ITEM should be used, on the right side with examples.

entry.title: Re: Overwrite dimension filters in SAC Add-in with date r...
entry.content:
@Jonas89 

I tried to simulate something and got it working. may be you can try at your end. As I said what SAP.OVERWRITEDIMENSIONFILTER supports is a Excel range for value range and not the value range directly. So for passing a date range you can try the below Hack.

In Excel cell Create a start date and End date cells as shown below.

In a hidden column write the below formula which will generate an array of dates between these 2 dates.

Formula for inclusive dates ( including first and last day): =TEXT(D3+SEQUENCE(1+D4-D3,,0),"YYYY-MM-dd")

Formula for exclusive dates ( excluding first and last day): =TEXT(D3+SEQUENCE(D4-D3-1,,1),"YYYY-MM-dd")

Now just pass this dynamic array to the SAP.OVERWRITEDIMENSIONFILTER formula.

=SAP.OVERWRITEDIMENSIONFILTER(Q4,"Ship_Date",A9#,0,1) A9 is the first cell of the dynamic array.

Results

Hope this helps

Nikhil

entry.title: "Certification Elevated My Confidence and Brand": How Rudy’s Commitment to Learning Inspires Others
entry.content:
Rudy Abrahams describes himself as “an introvert who is not shy” — a lifelong learner, activist for equity and equality, and a passionate advocate for healthy living. Growing up in humble beginnings in South Africa, Rudy’s early life shaped the values he holds today: resilience, fairness, and a deep belief in continuous self-improvement. Now an SAP employee based in Singapore, he balances his high-paced career with family life, proudly married to Dorianne for 25 years and raising two sons. 

“I love anything that includes healthy living (in all aspects of health),” Rudy shares. “And I’m a lifelong learner — it’s something I do naturally.” 

A Leadership Journey Across Continents 

Since joining SAP in 2017, Rudy has embraced change, leadership, and growth at every step. Over just eight years, he has held five major roles across five countries, including Head of Presales in EMEA, Head of Digital Transformation Office in Southeast Asia, Head of SAP SuccessFactors in SEA, and President & Country MD in the Philippines. Today, he serves as Head of Industry & Value Advisory for SAP APAC. 

Through it all, Rudy credits his success to self-discipline and focus. His daily routine starts early: "I rise at 5am to meditate, then 45 minutes of exercise, and I start work at 8am. I'm flat out until 6pm daily." His workday is filled with customer engagements, leadership meetings, and supporting SAP’s Customer Advisory Team. 

Leading by Example with SAP Certification 

But despite his busy schedule, personal growth and leading by example are high priorities for Rudy. True to his leadership style, he believes in setting the standard for his team. “I’m not willing to ask my team to do anything I’m not willing to do myself,” he says. 

Rudy Abrahams, Head of Industry & Value Advisory for SAP APACThis was just one of the reasons Rudy made the decision to pursue an SAP Certification. He saw it as an opportunity to inspire through action while enhancing his own skillset. Although the certification was not mandatory for Rudy, he likes to lead from the front. Closing skills gaps starts with leaders who commit to learning, and certified teams drive better outcomes – it's that simple,” Rudy explains. "I also know that this certification is an investment in my credibility and knowledge." 

He chose to certify in Positioning SAP Business Suite, one of SAP’s newest offerings for all SAP employees and partners. It includes practical exercises that build skills to help position SAP Business Suite, so was the ideal choice to strengthen Rudy’s product and strategic knowledge. But the journey wasn’t always smooth. 

“It was fun upfront, but the exam was fairly tough for me,” he admits. After failing his first attempt, Rudy didn’t give up. “I was disappointed but motivated to pass, so I decided to study hard and rebooked my test.” Passing the exam brought a renewed sense of confidence: “I feel much more confident and liberated now, especially knowing this was not an easy certificate to secure. I feel beyond proud.” 

Transforming Confidence into Connections 

The benefits of certification extended far beyond product-related skills. Rudy found that it increased his visibility and credibility within SAP. “I have elevated my brand in our business,” he says. “I met new colleagues who walked up to me and asked me for tips and insights.” This unexpected networking has created new friendships and stronger professional bonds.  

Beyond professional recognition, the experience boosted Rudy’s personal sense of achievement. "It made me feel like I could achieve just about anything." What’s more, Rudy's example has inspired others to follow his lead: “Many of my colleagues have turned to me and told me they are motivated to do their certification too. It’s a win-win for all, I guess.” 

A Lifelong Learning Mindset 

For Rudy, the journey doesn't stop here. Learning has always been a way of life — a philosophy he’s lived through every chapter of his career and personal growth. Although it requires dedication, time and motivation, he believes it's well worth the investment. “Lifelong learning isn’t a choice anymore, it’s a mindset.” 

As SAP and the wider technology world continue to evolve, Rudy’s story is a reminder that investing in yourself — even when it’s tough — opens new doors, builds new relationships, and paves the way for even greater success. 

Start your own success story    

Did you feel inspired by Rudy’s story? Then kick-start your own success story with SAP today. Benefit from tailored and self-paced learning opportunities that meet you when and where you learn best on SAP Learning site. Get certified to demonstrate your expertise and boost your career.

entry.title: Re: Insert multiple keyword into a Category via Impex
entry.content:
The issue you are facing might be due to missing unique modifier. Given that there are two keywords for same category, impex might create new row and update it with new data.

For newer commerce versions following insert_update works by creating two separate entries:

INSERT_UPDATE Category2KeywordRelation;$category[unique=true];$keyword[unique=true];$lang
;goggles;testa:en
;goggles;testb:en

entry.title: Re: B2BUnit and B2bcustomer and their assignment
entry.content:
To answer your questions:

  1. How B2BUnit and B2BCustomer Assignment Works:
    B2BUnits are organizational entities in B2B commerce representing a business partner company. B2BCustomers are individual users belonging to these units.

    In Backoffice, you can assign B2BCustomers to B2BUnits directly. This assignment is straightforward through the user interface where you can associate a customer to an organizational unit.

  2. Are Many B2BCustomer Assignments Possible?:
    Yes, a B2BUnit can have multiple B2BCustomers assigned to it. Each B2BCustomer can belong to exactly one B2BUnit reflecting the organizational structure of the business partner.

  3. What is the Role of B2BUserGroup? Are They Mandatory?:
    B2BUserGroups are similar to regular user groups but tailored for B2B scenarios. They encapsulate roles and permissions that can be assigned to B2BCustomers to define their functional capabilities and access rights within the e-commerce system.

    They are not mandatory, but they are essential for managing permissions and roles. For security and functionality purposes, users should be grouped appropriately to ensure that they have the correct access levels.

  4. B2BCustomer is Called User in B2B Data Model?:

    In SAP Commerce Cloud, the B2BCustomer extends the User model. It inherits the properties and behaviors of a User but introduces additional attributes specific to B2B scenarios like company affiliation and roles.

  5. User Groups are Also Called Roles in B2B?:
    Yes, user groups align closely with the concept of roles. In B2B commerce, these user groups often map to business-specific roles that users have within an organization, defining their responsibilities and access within the platform.

  6. Why Are There Two Commerce Objects for the Same Person (B2BCustomer and Customer)?:

    The distinction reflects different business models: B2B for business transactions and B2C for individual or consumer transactions. While a profile can potentially belong to both models, they are kept separate to maintain clear operational boundaries and functionalities.

    This separation allows the system to differentiate transactional processes, business rules, and customization specific to B2B and B2C contexts.

  7. More Relationships Between B2BUnit and a Customer:

    SAP Commerce Cloud allows extension of the data model. If additional relationships or attributes are needed between B2BUnit and customers, you can customize the model. OOTB, the primary relationship is direct assignment to one unit.

    You can define custom attributes or create new associations based on your business requirements.

  8. Why Doesn’t Commerce Have a Contact Model in B2B Like in S/4 and C4 Org/Account?:

    SAP Commerce Cloud focuses more on digital commerce capabilities rather than CRM functionalities, which S/4 and SAP C4C handle.

    Commerce Cloud can be integrated with CRM systems where such contact models exist to complement its functionalities with CRM-specific capabilities.

entry.title: Re: Custom entity, $count, $skip, $top not supported
entry.content:
Hi all,

thanks for all your inputs. Finally I was able to solve this issue. As it took me several days to find, I want to share with you.

First of all, I used Postman to do the call by myself. As said, when I remove $top etc., it worked. If I add it again, I noticed that it provides an exception class as part of the error message, which is /IWCOR/CX_OD_URI_SYNTAX_ERROR.

So I've put a break point to the constructor of this exception and I've noticed it gets raised in method  validate_system_query_operation of class /iwcor/cl_od_uri

  METHOD validate_system_query_option.

* OASIS Specification odata-v4.0-wd02-part2-url-conventions-final-2013-06-24.docx, Chapter 5.1
* For GET requests, the following rules apply:
* - Resource paths identifying a single entity, a complex type instance, a collection of entities, or a collection of complex type instances allow $expand and $select.
* - Resource paths identifying a collection allow $filter, $count, $orderby, $skip, and $top. TODO what about skiptoken?
* - Resource paths identifying a collection of entities allow $search.
* - Resource paths not ending in /$count or /$batch allow $format.
*
* For POST requests to an action URL, the return type of the action determines the applicable system query options, following the same rules as GET requests.
* POST requests to entity sets as well as all PUT and DELETE requests do not allow system query options (in OData V4)
* - systemQueryOption = '$id,$search,$filter,$count,$orderby,$skip,$top,$expand,$select,$format'.
* - entityOption      = '$expand,$format,$select'.
* This is not valid for OData V4.01:
* V4.01/protocol/11.4.1.5: returning of results from modification requests:
* modification requests MAY specify $expand and $select for single instance modifications.

    DATA lr_allowlist TYPE REF TO string.

    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_empty             TYPE string VALUE ''.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_service           TYPE string VALUE '$format'.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_service_all       TYPE string VALUE '$search,$count,$skip,$top,$skiptoken,$format,$apply,$select,$filter,$deltatoken'.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_service_all_cast  TYPE string VALUE '$search,$count,$skip,$top,$skiptoken,$format,$apply,$select,$filter,$deltatoken'.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_metadata          TYPE string VALUE '$format'.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_batch             TYPE string VALUE ''.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_crossjoin         TYPE string VALUE '$search,$count,$skip,$skiptoken,$top,$format,$filter,$apply,$orderby,$expand,$select'.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_entity_id         TYPE string VALUE '$id,$expand,$select,$format'.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_no_return_type    TYPE string VALUE '$format'.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_entity_set        TYPE string VALUE '$search,$count,$skip,$skiptoken,$top,$format,$filter,$apply,$orderby,$expand,$select,$deltatoken'.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_entity_set_ct     TYPE string VALUE '$search,$filter,$deltatoken'. " URL/5.1
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_entity            TYPE string VALUE '$expand,$select,$format'.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_media_stream      TYPE string VALUE ''.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_reference         TYPE string VALUE '$format'.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_reference_coll    TYPE string VALUE '$search,$filter,$count,$orderby,$skip,$skiptoken,$top,$format'.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_prop_comp         TYPE string VALUE '$expand,$select,$format'.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_prop_comp_coll    TYPE string VALUE '$apply,$filter,$count,$orderby,$skip,$skiptoken,$top,$expand,$select,$format'.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_prop_comp_coll_ct TYPE string VALUE '$filter'.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_prop_prim         TYPE string VALUE '$format'.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_prop_prim_coll    TYPE string VALUE '$filter,$count,$orderby,$skip,$skiptoken,$top,$format'.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_prop_prim_coll_ct TYPE string VALUE '$filter'.
    CONSTANTS lc_allowlist_prop_prim_val     TYPE string VALUE '$format'.

    CASE /iwcor/if_od_uri~uri_type.
        " Root Elements
      WHEN /iwcor/if_od_uri~gc_type_service.
        GET REFERENCE OF lc_allowlist_service           INTO lr_allowlist.
      WHEN /iwcor/if_od_uri~gc_type_service_all.
        GET REFERENCE OF lc_allowlist_service_all       INTO lr_allowlist.
      WHEN /iwcor/if_od_uri~gc_type_service_all_cast.
        GET REFERENCE OF lc_allowlist_service_all_cast  INTO lr_allowlist.
      WHEN /iwcor/if_od_uri~gc_type_metadata.
        GET REFERENCE OF lc_allowlist_metadata          INTO lr_allowlist.
      WHEN /iwcor/if_od_uri~gc_type_batch.
        GET REFERENCE OF lc_allowlist_batch             INTO lr_allowlist.
      WHEN /iwcor/if_od_uri~gc_type_crossjoin.
        GET REFERENCE OF lc_allowlist_crossjoin         INTO lr_allowlist.
      WHEN /iwcor/if_od_uri~gc_type_entity_id.
        GET REFERENCE OF lc_allowlist_entity_id         INTO lr_allowlist.
        " 100 - Resources without boundFunctions and without boundAction (Not starting with a FunctionImport)
      WHEN /iwcor/if_od_uri~gc_type_entity_set.
        GET REFERENCE OF lc_allowlist_entity_set        INTO lr_allowlist.
      WHEN /iwcor/if_od_uri~gc_type_entity_set_cnt.
        GET REFERENCE OF lc_allowlist_entity_set_ct     INTO lr_allowlist.
      WHEN /iwcor/if_od_uri~gc_type_entity.
        GET REFERENCE OF lc_allowlist_entity            INTO lr_allowlist.
      WHEN /iwcor/if_od_uri~gc_type_media_stream.
        GET REFERENCE OF lc_allowlist_media_stream      INTO lr_allowlist.

In my case, URI_TYPE was set to 100, which corresponds to /iwcor/if_od_uri~gc_type_entity.

You see, it gets handled as Entity and there was a small light bulb in my head turning on. If this is an Entity and not an EntitySet, $top, $count etc. really doesn't make sense at all.

So why was it taken as Entity instead of EntitySet? In UI annotation, I defined it as LINEITEM_REFERENCE, so might this not be an EntitySet?

Answer
NO, this gets defined by cardinality of CDS View. I defined it at 0..1, as in my special case, there can't be more than one record

                                                                    
  composition [0..1] of Z_CE_MFGORDOPERATIONWORKTIME  as _WorkTime

If I change this to [0..*], URI type is set to EntitySet, which allows all $count etc. for sure.

  composition [0..*] of Z_CE_MFGORDOPERATIONWORKTIME  as _WorkTime   //   on  _WorkTime.ManufacturingOrder = $projection.ManufacturingOrder

Conclusion
Always double check cardinallity and there is a really huge difference between 1 and  *

entry.title: Re: Rework required for Fiori apps exposed in SAP Workzon...
entry.content:
Hello @fvdlangenberg1 ! I see you are relatively new here so welcome to the SAP Community.

Some parts of your question are a little bit confusing but let me focus on the core of your question.

If you have an app in BTP (used in SAP BUILD WorkZone or not) which relies on an external source in a local network which is not exposed to the internet, you need the SAP cloud connector installed in some server on your local network connected with the BTP subaccount you are using. On that same BTP subaccount you'll need a destination which will be used inside your app (both in files xs-app.json to redirect some calls to it and likely in some ui5.yaml files that are needed if you run your app locally/in BAS.

SAP Cloud connector allows you to connect with non-SAP systems, which might be the case of your archive server. So in theory you wouldn't need an API to proxy those calls for you. Assuming you don't need an authentication which is not supported by Cloud Connector, I don't see any issues with having a destination pointing to your archive server via Cloud Connector.

Now it's confusing if you also need this app deployed in your local network or you only use it when developing locally. It's worth sharing here your manifest.json file containing the data sources as well as how are you fetching this files in your controllers. Sharing your ui5.yaml files and your xs-app.json might be helpful as well.

entry.title: How to Use Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA for Physical Inventory Management
entry.content:
Managing physical inventory efficiently ensures accurate stock levels and smooth warehouse operations. SAP S/4HANA introduces a powerful tool - Situation Handling - that enables proactive issue detection and response during the physical inventory process. With situation types like SPHYSINVMO, users can receive timely alerts about pending or delayed inventory activities, helping them to act swiftly and maintain stock accuracy. In this blog, we learn how to configure situation handling for physical inventory, assign responsible teams, and leverage SAP Fiori apps to stay ahead of operational issues.

Process Guide:

Step 1: Manage Teams and Responsibilities

Provide Name, Description, Global ID, status as Enabled and select search help on type.

Type: SPHYSINVMO - Physical Inventory Monitoring

Category: PHYSINVMO - Physical Inventory Monitoring

Navigate to the responsibility definitions tab and provide relevant data

Navigate to the team members and maintain them as team members

Maintain Functions

Team created

Step 2: Manage Situation Types (App: Manage Situation Type)

Choose the situation template as MAN_PHYSICAL_INVENTORY_MONITOR and proceed to copy

Provide ID, Name under the Admin Information

Under the Conditions tab, set your conditions 

In this case, the PI document must not be counted, must not be deleted, and the difference must not be posted.

Planned count date must be previous 14 Days, the next 3 days, and the plant must be 1000.

Maintain the Time zone and batch start time under the Batch Job Scheduling tab

Navigate to the Situation display tab, and mention the description in the notification

Navigate to the recipients tab, mention the responsibility and the Member function

Navigate to the Situation Monitoring tab, check for Monitor Instances and Capture Data Context and save.

To trigger the instances, click Trigger Now 

Step 3: Monitor Situations (App: Monitor Situations)

Step 4: My Situation in Function Member Account (App: My Situation)

Click on Line Item, and it navigates to Physical Inventory Document

 

Regards,

Leelamohan Mannem
